name: Sun-Dried Tomaotes & Roasted Peppers in Garli
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and dry the tomatoes, then cut them into eighths lengthwise.
2. Scoop out the seeds and juice from each tomato piece.
3. Place a wire rack on a baking sheet and arrange the tomatoes on the rack, skin side up. Allow to drain excess juice for about 1 hour.
4. Set the oven to warm (about 150°F). Transfer the rack to a clean baking sheet and place in the oven. Dry the tomatoes, turning them every few hours with a spatula until leathery, about 12 hours. Remove from the oven and let cool on the rack.
5. Preheat the broiler. Cut the peppers into halves lengthwise and discard the seeds.
6. Place the pepper halves skin side up on a baking sheet and broil them as close to the heat as possible until the skin chars, about 5 minutes.
7. Transfer the peppers to a brown paper bag and let cool for about 5 minutes. Rub off the skins and cut the peppers into 1/2-inch wide strips.
8. Prepare sterilized pint jars with garlic cloves and basil leaves.
9. Arrange a single layer of tomato strips in the bottom of each jar, followed by a layer of pepper strips. Add a garlic clove and a few basil leaves, keeping them against the side of the jar.
10. Continue layering until all jars are almost filled but not tightly packed.
11. Carefully pour olive oil into the jars until about half full. Tilt the jars and tap to allow the oil to fill any air bubbles and cover the vegetables completely.
12. Add more oil, covering the vegetables and leaving a 1/8-inch space at the top of the jars, then seal.
13. Store in a cool, dark place for at least a week before using and no longer than 3 months.
